name: Hyogo Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the seas surrounding Hyogo and the prefecture's clear skies
harmony and the prefecture's bright future

description: A light-blue field charged at the centre with a large white stylised hiragana ひ (hi), the first kana of the prefecture's name.
history: Hyogo's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and officially adopted on 11 September 1964. The flag, with the white symbol on a light-blue field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the light blue of the Seto Inland Sea and the Sea of Japan that flank Hyogo
a stylised hiragana ひ (hi), the first character of Hyogo (ひょうご / 兵庫); the rounded form symbolises the unity and harmony of the prefecture's people and the swelling waves of the surrounding seas.
